creator: chmeeedalf+freebsd@gmail.com
text:
When building devel/subversion using 'portmaster -dHg devel/subversion', it fails creating the package (succeeds building and installing subversion), with the following errors:

tar: share/subversion/client-side/svnmucc/.libs/svnmucc: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/dev/svnraisetreeconflict/.libs/svnraisetreeconflict: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/diff/.libs/diff: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/diff/.libs/diff3: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/diff/.libs/diff4: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/server-side/.libs/svn-populate-node-origins-index: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/server-side/.libs/svn-rep-sharing-stats: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: share/subversion/server-side/.libs/svnauthz-validate: Cannot stat: No such file or directory
tar: Error exit delayed from previous errors.
pkg_create: make_dist: tar command failed with code 256

Looking at pkg-plist, I see that there are a whole bunch of .libs/* entries, which look like they don't belong there, although they are installed somehow (don't appear in the install log).

How-To-Repeat: Build devel/subversion with portmaster -dHg or 'make package'
